name: Ziti with Ratatouille Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 small Japanese eggplants, cut crosswise into 1/4-inch pieces
1 large red bell pepper, cut into 4 or 5 large pieces
1 small sweet potato, peeled and diced
12 ounces ziti, or other tube-shaped pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large nonstick skillet, warm the oil over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking. Stir in the eggplant, bell peppers, zucchini, sweet potato, onion, and garlic. Stir-fry until the vegetables begin to soften, about 10 minutes. Stir in 2 tablespoons each of the basil and mint.
2. Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and cook until the vegetables are very tender, about 10 minutes.
3. Remove the large pieces of red bell pepper from the skillet and place them in a food processor. Add the 3/4 cup tomato juice and 3 of the canned tomatoes to the processor and process until smooth.
4. Return the pureed tomatoes, the vinegar, black pepper, sugar, and red pepper flakes to the skillet. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, breaking up the remaining tomatoes with a spoon. Reduce the heat to low and simmer while you cook the pasta.
5. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the ziti and cook until al dente, 10 to 12 minutes, or according to package directions.
6. Drain the pasta in a colander and transfer to individual dinner plates. Spoon the ratatouille sauce over the ziti. Sprinkle the remaining basil and mint on top and serve.
